C) Suppression windows / dedupe
- 14-day suppression per company per signal family (scale/reliability/compliance)
- If a task exists and is open, do not create another; update the existing task note instead
- Max 1 new task per company per 7d across ALL signal families (unless compliance signal is present)

D) Marketing touch suppression
- If marketing lifecycle email sent in last 72h, don’t create AE task unless threshold signal is “high” (>=$5k 30d or >=10M tokens/day)
- Add a property we can check: last_marketing_touch_ts (Tyler, can we stamp this via workflow?)

Tyler Benson: For D: yes, we can stamp something like `last_mktg_touch_date` on company record from HubSpot side. But if the router is creating tasks externally, we need it to read that property before task creation.

David Kim: That’s doable. The router already does company lookup for ownership. We can add a guard: if last_mktg_touch_date within X hours and signal_score < threshold => suppress. We’ll need agreement on which property + format.

Ben Carter: Re A: I’d be careful on hard $ thresholds because we’ll miss high-volume free-credit accounts that are clearly scaling. Maybe “$ OR tokens” as you wrote.
Also we should treat embeddings separately; some of the “tokens/day” logic doesn’t map cleanly.

Camila Reyes: Good call. Let’s split “generation tokens” vs “embedding units” in the scoring output and set distinct cut lines.

Isabelle Laurent: Suggest a simple prioritization rule for the task title/body:
- If compliance intent present => “Compliance / Private interest” (highest)
- Else if reliability (429s/latency) high => “Reliability risk / Dedicated candidate”
- Else scale => “Scale growth / Dedicated candidate”
Otherwise don’t route.

Avery Johnson: +1 on priority. Right now everything comes through as “PLG Signal: Intent” and it’s impossible to tell what’s real.

Stephanie Nguyen: Also: for accounts w/ an existing CSM owner, I’d rather CS be looped first (or at least tagged) vs AE creating new outreach. Can we add that as a suppression/route rule?

David Kim: We can check if `csm_owner` is set (or whatever property we use) and either (a) assign the task to CSM, or (b) create a task for AE but add CS as follower (HubSpot doesn’t have followers the same way, but we can create a second task or note). We should avoid doubling tasks.

Nikhil Sharma: Minimal version: if `csm_owner_id` present => route to CSM queue and create a note for AE owner only (no task). That avoids collisions.

Camila Reyes: Action items:
- Ben + Nikhil: propose updated intent definitions (2-event rule) by EOD tomorrow
- David: confirm feasible fields + read-guards (marketing touch + open task dedupe) + estimate
- Tyler: pick the “marketing touch” property name + workflow to stamp it
- Isabelle: draft the “task title/priority” mapping + share in thread
- Stephanie/Avery: keep a short list of the worst false positives (company names + what triggered) so we can validate suppression rules

Ben Carter: :thumbsup: I’ll also update the pilot dashboard to track “tasks/company” and “% suppressed” so we can see if changes are working.

Tyler Benson: One more thought: add an allowlist for the pilot (top N accounts by last-30d usage) until thresholds stabilize. That cuts blast radius.

Camila Reyes: I’m open to a temporary allowlist for NA week 2 if needed. Let’s try threshold + suppression first, but if we’re still >~40 tasks/week we should gate.

Isabelle Laurent: Here’s draft task mapping:
- Compliance: create task “PLG: Private/Compliance interest (review within 24h)”
- Reliability: “PLG: Reliability risk (429s/latency) — offer Dedicated / capacity review”
- Scale: “PLG: Scale growth — discuss Dedicated economics + throughput”
Include in body: signal summary, 7d metrics, links (docs viewed), and suppression reason if applicable.

David Kim: Noted. I’ll make sure the router writes a single consolidated note with:
- top_signal_family
- contributing_events
- window (7d/14d)
- recommended next step + playbook link

Avery Johnson: If we can get the “recommended next step” in the task, adoption will go way up.

Camila Reyes: Great. I’ll post an updated Week 2 plan in the pilot doc once we agree on exact cut lines. For now: please react :thumbsup: if you’re aligned with A/B/C/D above, or reply with edits.

title: #marketing: 1746134552
preview:
Camila Reyes: seeing a few early pilot accounts get *both* lifecycle nurture + AE outreach in the same 24h :grimacing: we need suppression rules before this scales. can we align today on what gets suppressed and for how long?

Tyler Benson: +1. we had a Quartz-ish complaint last week (not the same acct but same vibe). in HubSpot we can suppress marketing sends when a company enters a “Sales outreach in progress” state, but we need the rules to be explicit.

Ben Carter: yup. from Growth side, nurture is doing its job (docs/pricing/security views) but when the signal router fires and Sales jumps in, we can’t look uncoordinated. what are we optimizing for? (meeting booked rate + no spam)

Stephanie Nguyen: for the high-intent signals we want AE/SDR touch to win. if marketing hits them with a generic “here’s our dedicated guide” email after I’ve already sent something, it looks automated and cheap.

Grace O'Connor: agree. also from SDR cadence perspective: if they’re in an active sequence, marketing nurture can hurt deliverability and confuse threading.

Camila Reyes: ok, proposal for discussion:
1) If AE/SDR task is created from PLG signal → suppress non-transactional marketing emails to contacts at that company for X days.
2) If marketing email sent in last Y hours → delay SDR task creation by 1 biz day (or at least add a note so SDR doesn’t double tap).
3) Exceptions list (billing/receipt emails, product incident comms, etc.)
Thoughts on X/Y?

Isabelle Laurent: from ops/hygiene: we need something deterministic + auditable. otherwise we’ll get “why didn’t I get that nurture” questions forever. also: suppression should be at *company* level (domain) not just a single contact.

Tyler Benson: technically easiest is company property set by the router, e.g. `plg_sales_outreach_suppressed_until` + `plg_sales_outreach_owner`.
Then marketing workflows check “now < suppressed_until” and skip/unenroll from specific enrollments.

David Kim: we can do this with a custom company property + a workflow that unenrolls from selected marketing workflows. we need to list which workflows count as “nurture” vs “transactional”. also note: unenrollment is one-way unless we explicitly re-enroll later.

Ben Carter: I’m +1 on “AE task created → marketing suppression”. I’m less sure about “marketing last 48h → delay AE task” because for big signals we want speed.
Maybe: no delay, but tag the task w/ “Marketing touched in last 48h: {campaign}” so AE can reference it.

Stephanie Nguyen: yes please do NOT delay AE tasks on high score signals. speed matters.
But add a guardrail like “don’t send the generic dedicated upsell email if Sales is assigned.”

Grace O'Connor: also can we set a cap like “max 1 outbound touch per day per company” across both? even a loose one would help.

Tyler Benson: cross-team cap is hard unless we centralize in a sequencing tool. but we can approximate with:
- Marketing suppression when Sales is in active outreach
- Sales gets a note/visibility into recent marketing sends

Jasmine Liu: adding CS note: for existing customers, CSM should be aware before we suppress lifecycle comms. some of those emails are product education that reduces support load. can we scope suppression to only “upgrade prompts” vs all nurture?

Camila Reyes: good point. we should suppress only “upgrade / dedicated/private prompts” + “pricing oriented” sends, not general product tips or release notes.

David Kim: doable if we define the list. we can tag marketing workflows with an internal category (UpgradeNurture vs ProductEducation vs Transactional). then suppression only applies to UpgradeNurture.

Isabelle Laurent: re: durations. suggestion:
- Suppress UpgradeNurture for 14 days after Sales task creation OR until Sales marks outcome.
- If outcome = disqualified/false positive → suppression expires immediately (or 24h).
- If outcome = meeting booked/op created → suppression extends to 30 days (Sales-led motion).

Ben Carter: I like outcome-based. keeps us from permanently removing them from lifecycle.

Stephanie Nguyen: 14 days is fine. for meeting booked, 30 days makes sense (we’ll do our own sequencing + SE followups).

Grace O'Connor: can we add another rule: if contact replies “not interested” / unsubscribe, that should update suppression + stop both sides. just making sure we don’t keep poking.

Tyler Benson: that’s already standard unsubscribe handling for marketing, but Sales tasks won’t automatically stop. we can add a “do_not_outreach_plg” flag on company that Sales workflows respect.

Camila Reyes: ok summarizing proposed rules (v0):
- When PLG router creates Sales task on a company → set `plg_sales_outreach_suppressed_until = now + 14d`.
- Marketing suppresses ONLY workflows tagged UpgradeNurture while suppressed_until active.
- Router adds to HubSpot task note: “Recent marketing touches (last 7d)” (if available) so SDR/AE can avoid doubling.
- If Sales logs outcome:
  - Disqualified / false positive → set suppressed_until = now + 1d
  - Meeting booked / opp created → set suppressed_until = now + 30d
- Transactional + ProductEducation emails are not suppressed.
Any objections before we implement?

David Kim: implementation note: we can’t easily pull “recent marketing touches” into the task unless we query marketing events, but we can at least link to the company timeline view filtered to “Marketing email”.

Tyler Benson: +1. link is fine for MVP.

Ben Carter: can we also get a shared calendar of upcoming UpgradeNurture sends? even if suppression works, it’d help planning.

Camila Reyes: yes. request: Tyler can you set up a shared “GTM Touches” calendar (or a doc) that lists:
- upgrade nurture campaigns + send dates
- product launch emails
- known SDR blitz weeks
We’ll keep it lightweight but visible.

Tyler Benson: I can do a Google Calendar + a running sheet in the GTM drive. we can drop links in #sales and #marketing.

Stephanie Nguyen: please include the pilot cohort list so we don’t accidentally blast the same accounts.

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add a section for suppression rules + ownership in the HubSpot spec doc too.

Camila Reyes: perfect. David/Tyler: can you confirm ETA for the suppression workflow changes?

David Kim: if we keep it to 1 company property + workflow suppression for tagged UpgradeNurture, I can have it in staging tomorrow, prod by EOW (pending quick review).

Tyler Benson: I’ll share the calendar + sheet by EOD today.

Camila Reyes: great. I’ll post a short PSA once it’s live + where people should report conflicts.

title: #marketing: 1746058921
preview:
Ben Carter: Kicking off the regulated workloads Vertical ABM pilot thread. Goal today: lock kickoff agenda, attendee list, and links so we can move fast.

Ben Carter: Proposed kickoff (45 min):
1) Pilot goals + success metrics (10)
2) Vertical choice + ICP hypothesis (10)
3) Asset list + owners + deadlines (15)
4) Target accounts + routing NA/EMEA (5)
5) Weekly cadence + reporting (5)

Ben Carter: Quick question up front: do we start with 1 vertical (finserv) for weeks 1–4 and keep healthcare as optional week 5+ expansion, or try to run both from day 1?

Camila Reyes: +1 to starting with one. We’ll lose time if we don’t get crisp on claims + evidence process.

Soojin Lee: Agree. Finserv is cleaner first pass on “regulated” without triggering HIPAA language landmines in week 1.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: From Security POV: start with finserv. Evidence pack + questionnaire responses are already closer to bank/insurance asks (SOC2 scope, audit logs, KMS, retention).

Naomi Feldman: +1. Healthcare messaging needs extra guardrails (PHI boundaries, BAAs, “HIPAA-ready” wording). Better to do it right, not fast.

Ethan Wallace: Works for content too. We can get 1 landing page + finserv one-pager over the line first, then fork for healthcare.

Ben Carter: Ok — consensus: finserv first, healthcare as phase 2 if we hit prep milestones by end of week 2.

Ben Carter: Attendees for kickoff (pls confirm):
- Camila (sponsor)
- Ben (pilot lead)
- Soojin (PMM)
- Ethan (content)
- Tyler (ops/attribution)
- Grace (SDR)
- Markus (SE)
- Naomi + Aisha (Security)
- Stephanie (NA sales), Lukas (EMEA sales)

Stephanie Nguyen: Confirmed. I’ll bring a view on which accounts are realistically reachable + who we already have warm intros for.

Lukas Meyer: Confirmed. FYI EMEA finserv list will be smaller but higher intent (a couple already asked about VPC).

Markus Klein: Confirmed. If we’re committing to “fast security turnaround,” we need a clear intake path for questionnaires + a default “evidence pack index” link.

Naomi Feldman: Yep. We should standardize: first response = FAQ + evidence index + offer 30-min security review.

Elliot Price: Docs side: I can join kickoff for 10 min. If we’re publishing landing page updates, we need review checklist + what we can/can’t claim.

Ben Carter: Perfect.

Ben Carter: Links / working docs (drop anything I missed):
- Internal brief (Confluence): /sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/vertical-abm-pilot-regulated-workloads-brief
- Finserv messaging framework v1: /sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/finserv-messaging-framework-v1
- Regulated workloads security FAQ: /sales-enablement/security-faq/regulated-workloads-security-faq
- Evidence pack index: /security-and-compliance/compliance-evidence/abm-pilot-security-evidence-pack-index
- Week-by-week plan (Drive): /go-to-market/abm-pilot-week-by-week-plan
- Target account list (Drive): /go-to-market/target-account-list-finserv-health-sheet
- Landing page draft copy (Drive): /go-to-market/landing-page-copy-draft-regulated

Tyler Benson: For metrics, I’ll use the spec here: /go-to-market/abm-pilot-metrics-dashboard-spec — will wire to HubSpot + UTM conventions. Need final CTA so I can set conversion events.

Grace O'Connor: For SDR sequencing, I’ll need final “approved language” block from Soojin/Security so we don’t have 12 variants floating around.

Soojin Lee: I’ll post “approved vs do-not-say” language after today. Biggest thing: no “HIPAA compliant” or “guaranteed residency” statements. We can say “supports customer-managed retention policies” etc.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: +1. Also no “zero data retention” as a blanket claim. We should be explicit that retention is configurable by deployment mode + contract.

Kira Thompson: I can take the action to make sure landing page links to the FAQ + evidence index and that wording matches Security. (We can keep it conservative and point to a live security review.)

Ben Carter: Owner callouts (initial draft):
- Messaging final pass: Soojin + Naomi (Aisha for final approval)
- Landing page copy: Ethan (review Soojin/Security/Docs)
- Landing page publish mechanics: Elliot + Kira
- Finserv one-pager: Ethan + Soojin
- Outbound email sequence: Grace + Ben (Soojin/Security review)
- Target account hygiene + routing: Stephanie/Lukas + Tyler
- Demo / pilot outline: Markus (with SE team)

Camila Reyes: Looks right. Add a “single source of truth” folder for what’s externally shareable vs internal-only.

Naomi Feldman: +1000. Evidence artifacts need restricted sharing. We should only share via approved path, not random attachments.

Ben Carter: Weekly cadence: proposing 30 min standup Tue 10:00am PT / 1:00pm ET (catches NA), plus async EMEA notes. Alternative is Wed 9:00am PT. Preferences?

Stephanie Nguyen: Tue 10am PT works.

Grace O'Connor: Tue 10am PT works.

Tyler Benson: Tue 10am PT works. I’ll post weekly dashboard update an hour before.

Lukas Meyer: Tue 10am PT is 19:00 for me, not ideal but doable short-term. If we can keep it to 25 min and do EMEA follow-ups async, ok.

Camila Reyes: Let’s do Tue 10am PT for the pilot, keep it tight. We’ll do a separate EMEA micro-sync if it becomes painful.

Ben Carter: Locked: Tue 10am PT standup. I’ll send the calendar invite.

Ethan Wallace: For kickoff, do we want the meeting to include a quick “what we’re *not* building” reminder? Helps prevent scope creep from security asks.

Camila Reyes: Yes. Explicitly: no major features; docs/packaging clarifications only. Anything else goes into roadmap triage.

Markus Klein: :thumbsup: Also request: 5 min on “fast path” for security questionnaires (who answers what, expected SLA). That’ll save us later.

Ben Carter: Added to agenda under governance.

Elliot Price: If we’re publishing landing page in week 2, please tag me early for the review queue. Last minute = risky.

Soojin Lee: Noted. I’ll keep wording conservative and route for security approval before it hits PR.

Ben Carter: Next steps:
- I’ll post the kickoff calendar + agenda doc in this thread.
- Soojin/Naomi/Aisha: drop approved language block by EOD tomorrow.
- Ethan: landing page outline + finserv one-pager skeleton by Fri.
- Tyler: initial dashboard + UTM naming by Fri.

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: Thanks all. Keep this thread as the “kickoff command center” until we move into weekly standups.

Ben Carter: (reaction summary) Camila Reyes added :thumbsup: to Ben Carter’s message
Ben Carter: Cool — see you all at kickoff.

Maya Kapoor: Drafting a one-pager for enterprise buyers (security + SLA + deployment modes). Needs: one-page PDF, compact website block, and webinar. Keep copy tight for procurement teams. Any must-haves?

Ben Carter: Headline idea: 'Secure, SLA-backed LLM inference across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.' Then 3 short bullets per pillar and one benchmark callout.

Samir Gupta: Routing rules: route to AE if compliance_needed == true OR annual_commit >= 50k. Otherwise self-serve with sales opt-in. Add fields: deployment_preference, compliance_needed, expected_token_volume. Example lead JSON: `\{\"deployment_preference\":\"Hosted|Dedicated|Private\",\"compliance_needed\":true,\"expected_token_volume\":\"number\"\}`

Lena Fox: Legal check: wording must be careful. Say "supports customer-managed KMS, VPC/private control plane, and audit logs." For SLAs use "SLA-backed uptime subject to standard exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure)." Do not mention certifications unless validated.

Devon Park: Benchmark blurb suggestion: "Internal lab tests: Dedicated + quantization profiles reduced unit cost by ~20-35% at sustained high throughput; typical p99 gen latency observed 110-150ms depending on model." Call out workload: embeddings + reranking. Keep "internal tests" qualifier.

Iris Novak: Webinar tie-in: target Dec 2, 30-min (20-min demo + 10-min Q&A). Dry run Dec 1. Promo channels: blog, targeted emails to trial users, in-product banner. I'll draft the registration page copy.

Ben Carter: Visuals: small icons (shield, clock, cloud with lock) + tiny chart for cost per 1M tokens (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Self-host). Keep PDF one-column.

Samir Gupta: AE playbook bullets: typical contracting timeline 30-60 days for Dedicated + security review; required docs: DPA, network diagram, PO/contract. Who to loop: Solutions Eng, Security.

Lena Fox: I'll run the final copy for legal sign-off before publish. Also add a short compliance checklist block: encryption at rest, customer KMS, HSM option, audit logs, VPC peering.

title: #marketing: 1723509876
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync on benchmark webinar + launch playbook. Focus: unit economics (tokens -> $), latency tradeoffs, and routing. Need 3 simple charts, two messaging tracks (startup vs enterprise), and a clear inbound lead flow. Can we sketch a minimal deck outline?

Jonah Patel: Love it. My three-chart shortlist: 1) Cost per 1M tokens by model family (bars, call out batching/quantization assumptions), 2) p95 latency comparison by deployment mode (hosted / dedicated / private) as small multiples, 3) Throughput vs cost curve: tokens/sec at target p95 SLO with fallback strategies annotated.

Samira Rao: For chart 1 let's use model buckets: small (<2B), medium (2-20B), large (20-70B), xlarge (70B+). Show median cost and a light error band. Callout under chart: "assumes 8x batching + int8 quantization where supported".

Maya Li: Messaging: startups need a quick win tagline: Run a 5-min micro-benchmark and get tokens->$ in your app. Enterprise needs confidence tagline: SLA-backed latency + predictable unit economics for budgeting. Two CTAs to A/B.

Evan Brooks: Lead routing rules: If registrant runs the benchmark kit -> route to SDR with "warm" flag and include their chosen deployment preference. If they only attend -> automated 3-email drip with 1 short case study each (startup, PLG, enterprise) + invite to demo. Track which CTA clicked.

Ava Chen: Can someone draft punchy chart copy (headline + 1-line takeaway) so design can mock up? Also need a 90s demo script tied to the charts.

Samira Rao: Draft chart copy below:
- Headline: Spending clarity: cost per 1M tokens across model families
  Takeaway: Batching + quantization can reduce inference spend by up to ~6x for many workloads.
- Headline: Tail latency by deployment mode (p95)
  Takeaway: Dedicated capacity delivers consistent tail behavior; hosted is best for quick scale.
- Headline: Throughput economics at target SLO
  Takeaway: Smart routing and fallbacks let you maintain SLOs while cutting cost during spikes (~30% savings in common cases).

Jonah Patel: Add a small example calc pane beneath chart 1:
Example calc: `1M tokens = 2k calls * 500 tokens avg -> hosted baseline $120 -> batching+quantize ~$40`
(illustrative numbers only).

Maya Li: CTA variants:
- Variant S (startups): "Run a free micro-benchmark — see your tokens-to-cost in <5 min"
- Variant E (enterprise): "Request a capacity consult — predictable pricing & SLA"
Map CTRs to routing: S -> product-led nurture path; E -> AE/SE outreach.

Evan Brooks: Tracking KPIs post-webinar: micro-benchmark run rate, run->demo conversion, MQL->SQL velocity, demo requests by preferred deployment, time-to-first-run (goal <24h). Also capture bench-kit config to infer ops needs.

bench-bot: Summary saved: https://internal.redwood/bench/unit-econ-playbook. Attached: sample CSV, chart spec sheet. :robot_face:

Ava Chen: Owners: Samira = chart copy + CSV (due Wed), Jonah = design mocks (Thu), Maya = CTA copy + routing mapping (Thu), Evan = tracking spec + SDR playbook (Fri). I'll post 90s demo script v1 by EOD. Anything else?

Jonah Patel: Suggest one micro slide on "graceful degradation" for chart 3: show a spike where fallback routing switches to cheaper compatible variant and keeps p95 within SLO while reducing spend. One-liner under chart: "Fallbacks protect SLAs and limit cost spikes."

Maya Li: Good. We'll A/B test messaging that emphasizes "predictable budgeting" vs "lower unit cost" to see which drives enterprise interest.

Ava Chen: Great work team. I'll stitch this into the webinar outline once Samira's numbers land. Thanks! :thumbsup:
